build_check.description: Headless launch exits 0 without fatal errors; this 0/1 gate multiplies the rest.

requirements.description: Sunlight drops periodically onto the lawn and the player clicks to collect it into a visible resource counter. Plants cost sunlight to place; attempting to place without enough sun fails with a clear cue. 0 if there is no sun economy, if sun is never collected, or if plants are free. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Plants are placed on a grid of lanes by dragging from a toolbar. Each plant occupies one cell and cannot overlap. Plants remain stationary and perform their function automatically (shooting, blocking, producing sun). 0 if plants cannot be placed on a grid, if placement is not lane-based, or if plants do nothing after placement. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Enemies march from the right edge toward the left in lanes. They advance steadily, attack plants they encounter, and reaching the left edge causes a loss condition. Enemies have HP that decreases from plant attacks. 0 if enemies do not advance, if they ignore plants, or if reaching the edge has no consequence. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Enemies arrive in distinct waves with increasing difficulty. A wave indicator shows current wave and total waves. Defeating all waves in a level triggers victory. 0 if enemies arrive continuously with no wave structure or if there is no win condition. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

The full loop connects: title -> level select/adventure map -> gameplay -> victory/defeat -> return to map. Victory unlocks the next level. Defeat allows retry. 0 if any transition is missing or if progression is absent. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

At least four distinct plant types exist with different sprites and roles: a shooter, a sun producer, a wall/blocker, and at least one specialty plant (area damage, slow, or instant-kill). 0 if fewer than three types exist or if types are visually identical. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

At least three distinct enemy types exist with different sprites and behaviors: a basic walker, an armored type that resists damage, and a fast type that moves quickly. 0 if only one enemy type exists or if types behave identically. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

An adventure map or level select shows at least five playable levels with visible progression (locked/unlocked states, star ratings, or path connections). Later levels introduce new enemy types or conditions. 0 if there is only one level or no progression structure. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The lawn grid has at least five lanes and enough columns for meaningful placement strategy. Lane independence means enemies in one lane do not affect another unless a plant has cross-lane abilities. 0 if there are fewer than three lanes or if the grid is too small for tactical choice. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

New plant types are unlocked through level progression, expanding the player's toolkit over time. The player selects a loadout of available plants before each level. 0 if all plants are available from the start with no unlock system or if there is no loadout selection. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The HUD shows current sunlight count, wave progress, and available plants with their costs in clear non-overlapping regions. The player always knows what they can afford and how many waves remain. 0 if sun count, wave info, or plant costs are missing. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

The lawn grid is clearly rendered with visible lane divisions. Plants, enemies, and projectiles are mutually distinguishable. The toolbar and play area are visually separated. 0 if lanes are invisible, if plants and enemies look identical, or if UI overlaps gameplay. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Actions produce visible feedback: collecting sun shows it fly to the counter, placing a plant shows it appear on the grid, shooting shows projectiles traveling, enemy damage shows HP reduction, and enemy death shows a removal effect. 0 if actions produce no observable change. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Overall art coherence: lawn, plants, enemies, projectiles, UI, and result screens belong to a consistent garden-defense visual language with a bright, lively palette. 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

Plant and enemy sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art characters with distinct silhouettes and personality. Different types are immediately recognizable. 0 if plants or enemies are plain colored shapes. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

The lawn uses real textured grass tiles with row variation, and the background shows a house or fence establishing the setting. 0 if the lawn is a uniform green rectangle or if there is no background context. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets: plant cards have illustrated frames with sun costs, the adventure map has themed nodes, and result screens have authored chrome. 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

Animation and atmosphere: plants animate when attacking, enemies walk with movement cycles, projectiles travel visibly, sun floats down, and deaths have effects. The lawn has ambient life (swaying plants, drifting clouds). 0 if all elements are static with no animation. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.
